danish_text_clean:
I Norden er der ofte tung kritik af Gianni Infantino, fodboldens globale leder. Men i resten af verden er han så populær, at han styrer sikkert mod fire år mere som Fifa-præsident, skriver Jeppe Laursen Brock i denne analyse.

Mens kritikerne råber op, tæller Infantino stemmerne. De peger på fire år mere som fodboldens præsident.

Det begynder at runge af en historie, vi har hørt før.

Selv om der først er præsidentvalg i Det Internationale Fodboldforbund (Fifa) næste år, tegner der sig allerede nu et billede af, hvem der ender på den ærefulde, vellønnede og vigtige post: Gianni Infantino. Igen.
english_translation:
In the Nordic countries, there is often heavy criticism of Gianni Infantino, football's global leader. But in the rest of the world, he is so popular that he is safely heading towards four more years as FIFA president, writes Jeppe Laursen Brock in this analysis.

While the critics are speaking up, Infantino is counting the votes. They point to four more years as football's president.

It is beginning to echo of a story we have heard before.

Even though the presidential election in the International Football Federation (FIFA) is not until next year, a picture is already forming now of who will end up in the prestigious, well-paid, and important position: Gianni Infantino. Again.

vocabulary.example_from_article: I Norden er der ofte tung kritik af Gianni Infantino
vocabulary.note: Used figuratively here — not physical weight, but serious or grave criticism. A useful false-friend trap: 'tung' never means 'tongue'.

vocabulary.example_from_article: at han styrer sikkert mod fire år mere som Fifa-præsident
vocabulary.note: 'Styrer mod' = 'is heading towards'. Core meaning is to steer or be in control of something.

vocabulary.english: speaks up, cries out, makes noise
vocabulary.example_from_article: Mens kritikerne råber op, tæller Infantino stemmerne
vocabulary.note: Phrasal verb: 'råbe' alone means 'to shout/call'. Adding 'op' shifts the meaning toward raising one's voice in protest or making oneself heard.
Irregular in the past: 'talte' (not 'tællede'). Also used figuratively: 'Det tæller' = 'That counts/matters'.
Dual meaning: 'stemme' is both 'vote' and 'voice'. In an election context it means votes; in a choir or crowd it means voices. Context always clarifies.

vocabulary.example_from_article: Det begynder at runge af en historie, vi har hørt før
vocabulary.note: 'Runge af' means 'to echo of/with something'. The idiom here suggests the situation feels familiar — it rings of a story already known.

vocabulary.example_from_article: Selv om der først er præsidentvalg i Det Internationale Fodboldforbund (Fifa) næste år
vocabulary.note: Classic Danish compound: 'præsident' + 'valg' (election/choice). Danish compounds are always written as one word — watch for this pattern.

vocabulary.example_from_article: tegner der sig allerede nu et billede af, hvem der ender på den ærefulde, vellønnede og vigtige post
vocabulary.note: Reflexive construction: 'tegne sig' literally means 'to draw itself'. Used idiomatically in 'der tegner sig et billede' = 'a picture is forming/emerging'.

vocabulary.example_from_article: hvem der ender på den ærefulde, vellønnede og vigtige post
vocabulary.note: From 'ære' (honor) + '-fuld' (-ful). The '-e' ending appears because the adjective follows a definite article ('den'). Indefinite form: 'en ærefuld post'.

vocabulary.example_from_article: hvem der ender på den ærefulde, vellønnede og vigtige post
vocabulary.note: Compound adjective: 'vel-' (well) + 'lønnet' (paid/salaried, from 'løn' = salary). The '-e' ending is the definite/plural form.

grammar_notes.topic: V2 rule: inversion after a fronted clause
grammar_notes.explanation: In Danish the finite verb must always be the second element in the main clause. When the sentence opens with a subordinate clause ('Mens kritikerne råber op'), the subject and verb of the main clause swap positions: 'tæller Infantino' instead of 'Infantino tæller'. This inversion is mandatory and appears throughout the article.

'Selv om' introduces a concessive clause — it acknowledges a fact while pointing to a contrasting result. In 'Selv om der først er præsidentvalg næste år, tegner der sig allerede nu et billede...', the structure is: 'Even though X is true, Y is already the case'. Note that the verb in the main clause still inverts because the subordinate clause stands first.

Danish present perfect is built with 'have' (present: 'har') + past participle. 'Vi har hørt' = 'we have heard'. It is used here to connect a past experience to the present moment — a story already known, still relevant now. The past participle of 'høre' is 'hørt'.

'Sikkert' is the adverb form of adjective 'sikker' (safe, certain). In 'han styrer sikkert mod fire år mere', it modifies the verb 'styrer' and expresses confident certainty — he is surely/steadily heading that way. Many Danish adjectives form adverbs simply by adding '-t' to the neuter form.

discussion_questions: Hvorfor er der mere kritik af Infantino i Norden end i resten af verden, tror du?
Hvad synes du er vigtigt for en god leder af en stor organisation som Fifa?
Kender du andre eksempler på politikere eller ledere, der vinder valg på trods af kritik?
title: Mens kritikerne råber op, tæller Infantino stemmerne. De peger på fire år mere som fodboldens præsident
